faq.question: What should I do if the log reports unrecognised Home Connect API keys or values?
faq.answer: This message occurs when your appliance uses programs, options, or settings that are not yet known to the plugin. This is common because the Home Connect API frequently introduces new features or uses undocumented identifiers that vary between manufacturers and models. To resolve this and help improve the plugin: 1. Copy the technical snippet provided in the log (the section between the `=======` markers). 2. Click the GitHub link provided in the log message to open a new issue. The link is pre-configured to populate most of the required information. 3. Paste the snippet into the **Log File** field and submit the issue. Once reported, these keys are typically added in a subsequent release, allowing the plugin to correctly interpret and expose those specific appliance features to HomeKit.
faq.semantic_abstract: Home Connect API implementations frequently include undocumented program keys and settings that vary by appliance model and firmware version. The plugin utilizes a validation system to detect these unrecognised values, requiring user reports via provided log templates to map new functionality in subsequent updates.
